summary: glob-helper 2.0.0 postinstall exfiltrates host and filesystem data over HTTP
details: The package's postinstall.js reads host identity (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.homedir()) and probes filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, then opens an HTTP channel via require('http') to transmit the collected data. This shape — postinstall-time collection of host/user identifiers combined with filesystem enumeration and outbound HTTP — is a host-reconnaissance/exfiltration payload that runs on every `npm install` without any consumer action. The package name 'glob-helper' is also evocative of a utility library but the postinstall contains no build or native-addon justification for touching host identity or sending it over the network.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
